API Reference¶

pumpkinpipe.face¶

class pumpkinpipe.face.Face(landmarks, original_landmarks)[source]¶

Bases: object

draw()[source]¶
debug()[source]¶
class pumpkinpipe.face.FaceDetector(number_of_faces=1)[source]¶

Bases: object

find_faces(image, flip=False)[source]¶

pumpkinpipe.hand¶

Hand Detection Module

Author:

Nathan Forsyth

pumpkinpipe.hand.angle_3d(p1, p2)[source]¶

Returns the normalized 3D vector of 2 points.

Parameters:

  • p1 (list[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float]) – The 3D coordinates of the origin point.

  • p2 (list[float, float, float] | tuple[float, float, float]) – The 3D coordinates of the offset point

Returns:

Normalized 3D vector representing the angle between p1 and p2.

Return type:

tuple[float, float, float]

class pumpkinpipe.hand.Line(start, end, z, color)[source]¶

Bases: object

Dataclass for storing line points, colors, and z-index when drawing them for hand connections.

Variables:

  • start – The start point of the line in 2D pixel space.

  • end – The end point of the line in 2D pixel space.

  • z – The average z-index of the line, used for sorting the order to draw them in.

  • color – The color of the line.

Parameters:

  • start (Tuple[int, int])

  • end (Tuple[int, int])

  • z (float)

  • color (Tuple[int, int, int])

start: Tuple[int, int]¶
end: Tuple[int, int]¶
z: float¶
color: Tuple[int, int, int]¶
class pumpkinpipe.hand.Point(pos, z, color)[source]¶

Bases: object

Dataclass for storing landmark points, colors, and z-index when drawing them for hand connections.

Variables:

  • pos – The position of the landmark in 2D pixel space.

  • z – The z-index of the landmark, used for sorting the order to draw them in.

  • color – The color of the landmark.

Parameters:

  • pos (Tuple[int, int])

  • z (int)

  • color (Tuple[int, int, int])

pos: Tuple[int, int]¶
z: int¶
color: Tuple[int, int, int]¶
class pumpkinpipe.hand.Hand(landmarks, original_landmarks, side, box, image)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class for displaying and retrieving data for hands recognized by the HandDetector module.

Variables:

  • landmarks – List of landmarks using 3D pixel coordinates.

  • original_landmarks – List of the original landmark objects provided by the HandDetector.

  • side – String value representing the side of the hand (“Left” or “Right”).

  • thumb – 3D pixel coordinates of the thumb tip position.

  • index – 3D pixel coordinates of the index fingertip.

  • middle – 3D pixel coordinates of the middle fingertip.

  • ring – 3D pixel coordinates of the ring fingertip.

  • pinky – 3D pixel coordinates of the pinky fingertip.

  • wrist – 3D pixel coordinates of the wrist landmarker.

  • connection_style – Style settings for the color and thickness of the line connections of the hand when drawn.

  • landmark_style – Style settings for the color, stroke color, stroke thickness, and radius of the landmarks of the hand when drawn.

  • flags – List of binary values representing which fingers are up and which fingers are down.

  • box – The bounding box of the hand.

  • center – The 2D pixel coordinates center of the bounding box.

  • image – The image used by the HandDetector. Used as the default image to display the drawn hand.

  • DEFAULT_CONNECTION_STYLE – The default style settings for the color and thickness of the line connections of the hand when drawn.

  • DEFAULT_LANDMARK_STYLE – The default style settings for the color, stroke color, stroke thickness, and radius of the landmarks of the hand when drawn.

  • REGION_COLORS – The predefined colors for the different regions of the hand in BGR color space.

  • CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the whole hand.

  • PALM_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the palm.

  • THUMB_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the thumb.

  • INDEX_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the index finger.

  • MIDDLE_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the middle finger.

  • RING_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the ring finger.

  • PINKY_CONNECTIONS – A list containing all the Connection objects for the pinky finger.

  • THUMB_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the thumb landmarks.

  • INDEX_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the index finger landmarks.

  • MIDDLE_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the middle finger landmarks.

  • RING_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the ring finger landmarks.

  • PINKY_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the pinky finger landmarks.

  • PALM_LANDMARKS – A tuple of indices for the palm landmarks.

  • THUMB_TIP_ID – The index for the tip of the thumb landmark (4).

  • INDEX_TIP_ID – The index for the tip of the index finger landmark (8).

  • MIDDLE_TIP_ID – The index for the tip of the middle finger landmark (12).

  • RING_TIP_ID – The index for the tip of the ring finger landmark (16).

  • PINKY_TIP_ID – The index for the tip of the pinky finger landmark (20).

  • WRIST_ID – The index for the wrist (0).

Parameters:

  • landmarks (list[tuple[int, int, int]])

  • original_landmarks (list[object])

  • side (str)

  • box (BoundingBox)

  • image (ndarray)

DEFAULT_CONNECTION_STYLE: ConnectionStyle = <pumpkinpipe.utils.drawing.ConnectionStyle object>¶
DEFAULT_LANDMARK_STYLE: LandmarkStyle = <pumpkinpipe.utils.drawing.LandmarkStyle object>¶
REGION_COLORS: tuple[tuple[int, int, int]] = ((245, 135, 66), (245, 66, 167), (105, 66, 245), (66, 152, 245), (66, 245, 176), (127, 127, 127))¶
CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=0, end=1), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=1, end=5), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=9, end=13), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=13, end=17), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=5, end=9), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=0, end=17), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=1, end=2), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=2, end=3), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=3, end=4), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=5, end=6), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=6, end=7), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=7, end=8), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=9, end=10), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=10, end=11), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=11, end=12), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=13, end=14), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=14, end=15), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=15, end=16), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=17, end=18), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=18, end=19), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=19, end=20)]¶
PALM_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=0, end=1), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=1, end=5), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=9, end=13), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=13, end=17), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=5, end=9), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=0, end=17)]¶
THUMB_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=1, end=2), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=2, end=3), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=3, end=4)]¶
INDEX_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=5, end=6), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=6, end=7), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=7, end=8)]¶
MIDDLE_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=9, end=10), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=10, end=11), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=11, end=12)]¶
RING_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=13, end=14), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=14, end=15), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=15, end=16)]¶
PINKY_CONNECTIONS = [HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=17, end=18), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=18, end=19), HandLandmarksConnections.Connection(start=19, end=20)]¶
THUMB_LANDMARKS = (2, 3, 4)¶
INDEX_LANDMARKS = (6, 7, 8)¶
MIDDLE_LANDMARKS = (10, 11, 12)¶
RING_LANDMARKS = (14, 15, 16)¶
PINKY_LANDMARKS = (18, 19, 20)¶
PALM_LANDMARKS = (0, 1, 5, 9, 13, 17)¶
THUMB_TIP_ID = 4¶
INDEX_TIP_ID = 8¶
MIDDLE_TIP_ID = 12¶
RING_TIP_ID = 16¶
PINKY_TIP_ID = 20¶
WRIST_ID = 0¶
landmark_distance(landmark_index_1, landmark_index_2, image=None, draw=False)[source]¶

Finds the distance in pixels between 2 specified landmarks.

Parameters:

  • landmark_index_1 (int) – The landmark index for the first point

  • landmark_index_2 (int) – The landmark index for the second point

  • image (None | ndarray) – If not None, draws a line between the 2 points on the specified image

  • draw (bool) – If True, currently does nothing. Future implementations will draw the line.

Returns:

The distance between 2 points

Return type:

float

finger_flags()[source]¶

Finds which fingers are up and returns them as a binary list in the order of thumb, index, middle, ring, pinky.

Returns:

A list of binary values representing whether a finger is up or down

Return type:

list[int]

fingers_up()[source]¶

Provides a list of the fingers that are up in English.

Returns:

A list of finger names.

Return type:

list[str]

fingers_down()[source]¶

Provides a list of the fingers that are down in English.

Returns:

A list of finger names.

Return type:

list[str]

draw(image=None)[source]¶

Draws the hand skeleton on the specified image. Currently, this library uses a custom way to draw while drawing_utils.py remains unimplemented in the official mediapipe release.

Parameters:

image (None | ndarray) – The target image for the drawing. If none, it will draw on the hands self.image

debug(image=None, skeleton=True, bounding_box=True, center=True, side=True, fingers=True, flags=True, tip_points=True)[source]¶

Draws the requested debug information. Defaults to all debug information.

Parameters:

  • image (None | ndarray) – The image to draw the debug information on. If image is none then the hand will draw on its self.image

  • skeleton (bool) – When True, draw the landmarks and connections of the hand. Hand regions are separated by color.

  • bounding_box (bool) – When True, draw the outer bounding box of the hand.

  • center (bool) – When True, draw a green circle at the center of the bounding box. Also display the value for hand.center.

  • side (bool) – When True, write the value for hand.side underneath the hand.

  • flags (bool) – When True, display the value for hand.flags (binary list) representing which fingers are up or down.

  • fingers (bool) – When True, display the value returned by hand.fingers_up() (a list of strings for each finger that is registered as being up).

  • tip_points (bool) – When True, display hand.thumb, hand.index, hand.middle, hand.ring, hand.pinky, and hand.wrist values near their corresponding fingertips.

set_connection_style(stroke=None, thickness=None)[source]¶

Modifies the style of the hand connections when it is drawn.

Parameters:

  • stroke (None | tuple[int, int, int] | list[int, int, int]) – The BGR color of the connections.

  • thickness (None | float) – The thickness of the connector lines in pixels.

set_landmarks_style(fill=None, stroke=None, radius=None, thickness=None)[source]¶

Modifies the style of the hand landmarks when drawn.

Parameters:

  • fill (None | tuple[int, int, int] | list[int, int, int]) – The BGR color of the landmarks

  • stroke (None | tuple[int, int, int] | list[int, int, int]) – The BGR color of the outline of the landmarks

  • thickness (float | None) – Thickness of outline on circle

  • radius (float | None) – The radius of the landmarks

class pumpkinpipe.hand.HandDetector(max_hands=2)[source]¶

Bases: object

Class for setting up mediapipe and finding hand data.

Variables:

  • timestamp_ms – Variable for tracking time between frames.

  • frame_rate – Desired frame rate. Set to 30.

  • landmarker – Pipeline to detect hand landmarks.

Parameters:

max_hands (int)

find_hands(image, flip=False)[source]¶

Detect hands and add them to a list.

Parameters:

  • image (ndarray) – The image to detect hands in.

  • flip (bool) – When flip is True, handedness is reversed. “Left” becomes “Right” and vice versa.

Returns:

A list of detected hands.

Return type:

list[Hand]

pumpkinpipe.hand.main()[source]¶

Test script for the Hand Detection module.

pumpkinpipe.pose¶

pumpkinpipe.selfie_segmentation¶
